bead.title: execute-bead-loop: wire diagnose→remediate→rescan in idle path
bead.description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, emits loop.idle, and either returns (--once / drain) or sleeps (--watch). It never diagnoses or auto-remediates. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation requires the loop to diagnose every non-ready bead, fire matching remediators, and re-scan without sleeping when at least one remediation succeeded.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnose+dispatch logic exists at the insertion point.

PROPOSED FIX
Inside execute_bead_loop.go, after ReadyExecutionBreakdown populates breakdown (~line 1265) and before applyStop/sleepOrWake (~line 1271), insert the idle-path auto-remediation block.

Pseudo-flow (translate to Go):
1. Build childIndex, openChildIndex, depIndex from the store (these may already be computed inside ReadyExecutionBreakdown; expose them or rebuild as needed).
2. Walk breakdown.Epics ∪ breakdown.DepWaiting ∪ breakdown.OperatorAttention. For each bead, call bead.Diagnose(...) (from cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go).
3. Switch on the BlockReasonCode:
   - superseded_pending_close: queue a no-op (the cascade happens automatically inside any subsequent Close(); to actively trigger it on a known X, call store.Close on the SUPERSEDER Y if it's somehow still open in this branch — typically not; if X is the bead and Y is already closed but the cascade never ran, call a new store.RunSupersededCascade(X, Y) helper that runs the same scope-checked cascade out of band).
   - closure_candidate_misclassified: this should disappear once the RC-1 classifier fix lands; if it still appears, log and surface (defensive).
   - dead_intermediate_all_children_closed: trigger the existing closure-evaluation path against the bead.
   - dead_intermediate_open_children_pending OR epic_of_epic: skip (recursion via children handles it).
   - genuinely_needs_decomposition: queue an AutoDecomposeEpic call. At most ONE auto-decompose dispatch per loop iteration (track a counter). Skip if --no-auto-epic-decompose set.
   - dep_blocked_by, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis: no idle-path action; will surface via ddx work focus.
   - stale_graph_index: trigger an index rebuild and re-diagnose next loop.
4. Execute queued remediations sequentially. Track count of successes.
5. If any remediation succeeded, emit loop.auto_remediated event with {remediation_counts: {...}, total_attempted, total_succeeded}, clearTransientCandidateSkips, and `continue` (re-scan without entering sleepOrWake).
6. If none succeeded, fall through to the existing emit("loop.idle", ...) + sleepOrWake path.

Add operator override flags to cli/cmd/work.go: --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify. Plumb them through to the work loop via runtime config (mirror how existing --max-recovery-cost flows in).

Add config key work.autoRemediations: { autoSupersedeClose: bool, autoEpicDecompose: bool, autoClosureReclassify: bool } with defaults of true. Flags override config; per-project config overrides defaults.

Reuse existing cost budget threading: AutoDecomposeEpic counts against --max-recovery-cost just like the existing post-TooLarge decomposer does. Honor SmartRetryCooldown / SetExecutionCooldown precedent for per-bead cooldown after failed auto-decompose (already implemented in the auto_decompose_epic bead).

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the diagnoser, RC-1 fix, RC-2 cascade, RC-3 walk-up, RC-6 handler — those are separate beads this one depends on.
- Operator surface changes (ddx work focus, ddx work plan) — separate beads.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. Depends on ddx-79e66725 (diagnose), ddx-e9f3adaf (RC-1 lifecycle), ddx-04e89823 (RC-2 cascade), ddx-f500c75b (RC-3 walk-up), ddx-7b88e3a7 (RC-6 auto-decompose handler).
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go between the existing NoReadyWork branch and the sleepOrWake call hosts the diagnose→remediate→rescan logic described above.
2. cli/cmd/work.go exposes --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify flags; cli/internal/config defaults work.autoRemediations.{autoSupersedeClose,autoEpicDecompose,autoClosureReclassify} to true.
3. TestWorkIdle_AutoRemediatesAndRescansWithoutSleep: fixture with one mislabeled closure-candidate epic + one supersession leftover; ddx work --once auto-resolves both in a single loop iteration, emits loop.auto_remediated event with counts, no sleep, returns drained.
4. TestWorkIdle_AutoDecomposeUndecomposedEpic: fixture with one genuinely-undecomposed epic that satisfies all RC-6 preconditions; loop dispatches exactly one AutoDecomposeEpic call, child beads are filed, parent epic execution_eligible=false, loop re-scans and picks one child.
5. TestWorkIdle_AutoDecomposeRespectsCostBudget: --max-recovery-cost=0; loop diagnoses genuinely_needs_decomposition but does NOT dispatch; bead is surfaced as gated_by_budget_or_cooldown.
6. TestWorkIdle_AtMostOneDecomposePerIteration: fixture with three genuinely-undecomposed epics; one loop iteration dispatches exactly one decompose; subsequent iterations handle the remaining two.
7. TestWorkIdle_OverrideFlagSuppressesDecompose: --no-auto-epic-decompose set; loop does NOT dispatch even when all preconditions pass; bead is surfaced as gated_by_budget_or_cooldown with detail "no-auto-epic-decompose".
8. TestWorkIdle_NoSuccessFallsThroughToSleep: only beads with non-remediable diagnoses present; loop falls through to existing sleepOrWake path, emits loop.idle, behavior matches today.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1 green.
10. cd cli && go test ./... green.
11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
